title: cheiropleuria parva
intro: Cheiropleuria parva, also known as the small cheiropleuria or small finger fern, is a species of fern in the family Cheiropleuriaceae, native to East Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Cheiropleuria parva include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to improve skin health and reduce the risk of certain diseases. In herbal preparations, Cheiropleuria parva is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, and tonics that are said to promote overall well-being and boost the immune system.
